scope: Foundational Rust ownership extraction without compatibility facades

observed_owners.responsibility: external URL policy, resolved-address policy, and privacy-safe log labels
Job values, canonical normalization, and hashes without SQLx types
DNS-pinned outbound targets and bounded response-body reads
native paths, private file modes, package-smoke isolation, and database-key retrieval
unchanged migrations, SQLCipher connection, integrity, backup, and job repositories

verification.result: Security policy tests and the initial network target-resolution tests passed. The expanded network crate was then rerun independently.
51 unit tests and 5 documentation tests passed after Job, normalization, and hash ownership moved.
22 tests passed and one pre-existing environment-isolation test remained ignored; database-key validation and native path policy were included.
189 tests passed, including encrypted connection, plaintext upgrade, migration, backup, integrity, permissions, CRUD, search, and concurrency behavior.
8 DNS-resolution, body-limit, JSON, and error-redaction tests passed after bounded body reading moved.
The current explicit workspace and migration dependency graph compiled on the recorded macOS host.
Frontend and repository architecture contracts passed with the new units and edges.
8 topology contract tests passed, including the new deleted-index-path regression.
Every hard source and configuration limit passed; modularization review candidates remain reported as review work, not passes.

not_proven: removal of every raw SQLx pool consumer
completion of the application facade or remaining target crates
full workspace tests after the foundational extraction

native_windows_11_gap.missing_evidence: No native Windows 11 run from this working tree.
native_windows_11_gap.smallest_validation: Run pwsh -File ./init.ps1 and npm run test:scripts on a native Windows 11 host.

hosted_ci.canonical_status: nonconforming user override; no claim of canonical CI compliance
